---
title: "GavinK_pass2_comments"
output: html_document
---

The below contains comments written from my second pass through the book. My second pass was to tackle issues as outlined in `issues/Gavin-SummerWork.md`.

Note that when I quote text from the book, I remove certain things such as the r in "r pkg("package_name")" so that this doc will render properly.

# Chapter 3: Getting Started

> Tables are not usually designed to reveal *patterns, trends or anomalies*, although this can
be accomplished with modern table generating software such as the ` pkg("tinytable")` or `pkg("gt")` packages.[^tables]

- Maybe this is a good place to plug your `vcdExtra::color_table()` work

> See Friendly (2008); Friendly & Wainer (2021) for this history. .

- Extra dot needs removal

## 3.1 --- Why should you plot your data?

### 3.1.2 --- One lousy point can ruin your day

> Similarly, the regression lines showed the expected slope, $b \approx 1$ for men, but that for women was only 0.26.
It didn't make sense.

- Wondering if you want to change $b$ to $\hat{\beta}$
  + There is another example of this later on in the chapter ("$b = -0.231$")
